system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoSimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(8 9 10 11) 4(12 15 14 13) 4(8 12 13 9) 4(11 10 14 15) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run1/runs/squareBend/3/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 56.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Persistent Errors

We continue to see the same two errors after three attempts:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Block 2 `hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15)` has "inward-pointing faces"
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading error)

## Root Cause

The third attempt correctly identified that the issue is with **inward-pointing faces** in Block 2. The problem is specifically with the vertex ordering in the hex block definition. The current Block 2 is:

```
hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

With vertices:
- 8: (25, 0, 25), 9: (25, 0, -25), 13: (25, -75, -25), 12: (25, -75, 25)
- 11: (25, -50, 25), 10: (25, -50, -25), 14: (25, -125, -25), 15: (25, -125, 25)

**All vertices of Block 2 are at x=25!** This means Block 2 is degenerate — it collapses to a single plane (zero volume in the x-direction). That's why the faces are "inward-pointing."

## Correct Block Decomposition

The geometry needs to be re-examined. The outlet section should extend in the **negative x-direction** from the bend, not the positive x-direction. Looking at the vertex coordinates:

- The bend apex is around x=25 (vertices 8-11)
- The outlet extends to x=-500 (vertices 16-19)

So Block 2 should span from x=25 to x=-500, meaning it needs vertices at **both x=25 AND x=-500**. Currently, vertices 8-15 are ALL at x=25, and vertices 16-19 are at x=-500. We need to pair them properly.

The correct block topology should be:

- **Block 0** (inlet straight, x=-50 to x=0): `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — rectangular prism
- **Block 1** (bend, x=0 to x=25, y=-50 to y=25, z=-25 to z=25): `hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11)` — but vertex 10/11 are at y=-50, which is outside the y-range of Block 0. The bend block needs to properly connect the geometry.

Actually, re-reading the vertex definitions, the bend goes from y=-50 to y=25. The correct blocks should be:

- **Block 0** (inlet section, x=-50 to x=0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25): `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)` — proper rectangular prism with correct winding
- **Block 1** (transition/bend section, x=0 to x=25, y=-50..25, z=-25..25): `hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11)` — connects the top of Block 0 to the bend
- **Block 2** (outlet section, x=25 to x=-500, y=-125..-75, z=-25..25): `hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15)` — but this is STILL wrong because vertices 8-15 are all at x=25.

The fundamental problem is that the outlet vertices (16-19 at x=-500) are NOT being used in any block definition. And the outlet section should span from the bend region (around y=-50 to y=-125) back toward x=-500.

## Proposed Solution

**Redefine the blocks completely:**

- **Block 0** (inlet straight): `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — x=-50 to x=0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25
- **Block 1** (bend section): `hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11)` — x=0 to x=25, y=-50..25, z=-25..25  
- **Block 2** (outlet section): `hex (12 13 17 16 15 14 18 19)` — x=25 to x=-500, y=-125..-75, z=-25..25

Where:
- Block 2 uses vertices 12=(25,-75,25), 13=(25,-75,-25), 17=(-500,-75,-25), 16=(-500,-75,25), 15=(25,-125,25), 14=(25,-125,-25), 18=(-500,-125,-25), 19=(-500,-125,25)

This gives Block 2 proper extent from x=25 to x=-500 with y spanning -125 to -75 and z spanning -25 to 25.

**Update all boundary face definitions** to match the new block topology. The `inlet` face should be `(0 3 2 1)` (the face at x=-50), the `outlet` face should be `(16 19 18 17)` (the face at x=-500), and all wall patch faces should be updated accordingly.

## Error 2 Resolution

Once `blockMesh` succeeds, the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, `cells`) will be generated in `constant/polyMesh/`, and `log.rhoSimpleFoam` will resolve automatically.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace Block 2 hex definition from hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15) to hex (12 13 17 16 15 14 18 19) with grading (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Update outlet face to (16 19 18 17); Update upperWall faces to remove old ones and add (8 9 13 12) and (12 13 17 16); Update lowerWall faces to remove old ones and add (14 18 19 15) and (15 19 18 14); Update leftWall faces to keep existing (0 3 2 1) and (6 10 11 7) and replace (10 14 15 11) with (10 14 17 16) and (16 17 14 10); Update rightWall faces to keep existing (4 5 6 7) and (12 13 18 19) and replace (12 13 18 19) appropriately -- actually simplify by removing rightWall entry entirely since those faces are internal to the outlet block; Add mergePatchPairs if needed -- keep empty'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
